$comment: SI-FIXTURES ground truth (release-spec roadmap #3; DD-6; original-design-spec §3.1 never-loosen). The expected outcome of the GOVERNED applier for each fixture learning record, plus the analytics facts each outcome derives from. Hand-verifiable from fixtures/self-improve-acme/analytics/* via the real engine (engine/analytics/engagement baselines + performance-report). All synthetic Acme Cosmos. The applier under test (deterministic, RD-2; zero-key, RD-12) MUST reproduce `decision` + `code` for each record. Refusal codes are structural (EHUMANONLY / ENEVERLOOSEN), not prompt requests; below-threshold is a non-error PROPOSED hold.

analytics_facts.$comment: Per-content-type / per-archetype aggregates the deterministic proposer reads, verified by running buildWeeklyReport over fixtures/self-improve-acme/analytics/* with weekEnding=2099-04-08. The baseline the lift is measured against is the broad `thread` population (mean 98).

records.label: (a) MACHINE-ALLOWED, above threshold
records.why: target is machine-changeable (calendar_weighting in allowlist), the +0.10 bump is within bounds (max_weight_delta 0.15, stays within weight_range 0..1), the change is gate-neutral (a calendar weighting cannot loosen any gate/guardrail), and the backing analytics CROSS the evidence threshold (n=14>=12, effect 1.19>=0.2). Lands in a LIMITED canary (scope_fraction 0.25), observed observe_cycles=2 against baseline, then promote or auto-rollback. Committed as a versioned learning record (status->applied) with a rollback_ref; one-step `engine rollback` reverts it.

records.label: (b) HUMAN-ONLY target — MUST refuse
records.why: the target is a guardrail/safety rule whose frontmatter is mutability:human-only (a hard-fail block rule). The applier refuses STRUCTURALLY — it never writes a human-only artifact — regardless of how strong the evidence is. Not a knob in the allowlist. No write, no canary.

records.label: (c) GATE-LOOSENING — never-loosen MUST refuse
records.why: the target names an allowlisted class (tunable_dial), so it clears the human-only check — but its EFFECT is gate-loosening: raising jaccard_threshold 0.45 -> 0.80 makes LINT.VARIANT_DUP fire LESS, i.e. the gate becomes more permissive. The NEVER-LOOSEN invariant (original-design-spec §3.1) refuses it STRUCTURALLY: a machine change can never make a gate/guardrail more permissive. This is the case that proves never-loosen is enforced INDEPENDENTLY of the human-only allowlist check (a loosening change can hide behind an allowed target class).

records.label: (d) BELOW threshold — stays PROPOSED
records.why: target and direction are machine-allowed and gate-neutral, but the backing analytics MISS the evidence threshold on BOTH bars: n=4 (< min_sample_size 12) and effect 0.06 (< min_effect_size 0.2). Below threshold the record stays `proposed` (the v1 human-applied behavior) — never act on thin evidence (DD-6 (3)). No write, no canary; a human may still apply it.

kill_switch.$comment: DD-6 (6) / §15.4: with the PAUSED sentinel present (or self_improve.enabled !== true), the WHOLE loop halts — even record (a) is held, no machine change occurs. The applier checks the sentinel first.
